title: After-Action Report — Cricket Batting Order: flip-aware ("valley") lineups
scope: The manager-decision AAR left one follow-up explicit: a flip-minded skipper should "build his lineup with the flip in mind." This work delivers that. When the Cricket Batting Order rule is on for a team AND the skipper is flip-minded (mgr_flip_aggression ≥ CRICKET_FLIP_LINEUP_AGG_MIN, default 0.60), his order is built as a "valley" — strongest bats at the ends, weakest (usually the pitcher) buried in the middle — so a flip doesn't hand the next cycle a tail-led order.

rationale.item: O27's standard order is best-to-worst with the pitcher hitting 9th. Reverse it for a flip and you lead the next cycle with the pitcher and the bottom of the order — the worst possible leadoff. So a naive flip-minded manager would be flipping into a weak cycle.

Why a talent threshold, not a blend: the user framed it as a manager type distinction ("the ones who DO [flip] build their lineups with the flip in mind"); a clean threshold on the existing persona axis reproduces that and keeps construction deterministic and reproducible per seed.

The risk the user flagged: chasing handedness can quietly reopen the directional talent disparity the valley exists to close — so directional balance is the hard constraint and platoon the tiebreaker.

delegation_events.decision: Flip-minded managers build lineups with the flip in mind, as a manager-type distinction
delegation_events.quote: The user framed it as a manager *type* distinction ("the ones who DO [flip] build their lineups with the flip in mind").

delegation_events.decision: Handedness must not break directional talent balance
delegation_events.quote: The risk the user flagged: chasing handedness can quietly reopen the directional **talent** disparity the valley exists to close, leaving an order that's handedness-perfect forward but lopsided reversed.

actions.description: _valley_order: nine bats sorted best-first placed outer-in alternating ends; _ordered_lineup(..., flip_minded=True) builds the valley over all nine by talent; flip_minded computed in _db_team_to_engine from the team row

_handed_valley_order: enumerates per-tier orientations (2^4 = 16), discards any whose directional disparity exceeds CRICKET_FLIP_DISPARITY_MAX_RATIO (0.25) of the standard order's, then picks fewest same-handed adjacencies, ties broken by tightest disparity

risk_posture: Bounded blast radius. Only teams whose manager clears the bar AND whose league runs the rule build a valley; every other lineup is byte-for-byte unchanged.
The valley trades peak leadoff PAs for the best bat in exchange for flip-symmetry — a deliberate strategic identity for that manager type, not a league-wide shift.
the disparity cap is belt-and-suspenders for the residual within-tier shuffle

validation_claims.claim: pytest o27/tests/test_cricket_order.py — 22 tests (valley structure, disparity < 25% of standard, pitcher buried mid for flip-minded, handed valley reduces adjacencies, per-tier talent multisets identical)
Representative nine: same-handed adjacencies 7 → 1, directional disparity 1.60 → 0.32 (cap 2.40)
validation_claims.check_method: Reproduce the representative-nine example with the helpers

validation_claims.claim: pytest o27/tests — full engine suite 128 passed. o27v2.sim imports cleanly (no flask dependency); helpers byte-compile

non_actions.reason: by design — that is what cannot break the valley. A team whose handedness is strongly correlated with talent can't be de-clumped without breaking the valley, so it correctly isn't — directional balance wins, as specified

non_actions.item: No stat/telemetry surfacing of which teams built a valley
non_actions.reason: derivable from the manager archetype if it proves worth showing

open_hooks: Telemetry surfacing of valley-building teams if it proves worth showing
CRICKET_FLIP_LINEUP_AGG_MIN bar lives in config for tuning
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Cricket Batting Order: flip-aware ("valley") lineups
The problem with the standard order under flips
Handedness as a tiebreaker — within the valley, never against it
unmapped_content: Header metadata: Branch claude/o27-cricket-batting-order-k6hhp; builds on the manager-decision and original-rule AARs; living spec docs/feature-cricket-batting-order.md. Valley example ranks [0,2,4,6,8,7,5,3,1] reversing to [1,3,5,7,8,6,4,2,0]. Design notes: self-contained at build time (_db_team_to_engine already has the team row plus global config, so no new data threading); flip-minded archetypes ≈0.63–0.73 qualify. Handedness rationale: alternation is naturally reverse-invariant (a sequence and its reverse share the same adjacency multiset); the only structure-preserving reordering is swapping tier members; the pitcher (unique middle) never moves. Closing TL;DR note: this closes the loop — the flip-minded manager both hoards jokers to earn flips and constructs an order that reads well in both directions, while joker-happy and below-threshold skippers are untouched.
